sources.title: Kit Carson
sources.description: Title: Kit Carson Summary: Indian fighter, trapper and frontier scout Kit Carson leads a wagon train of settlers from Fort Bridger, along the Oregon Trail through Shoshone territory, to California which plans to secede from Mexico. Directed by: George B. Seitz Actors: Production Company: Edward Small Productions Release Date: 30 August 1940 (USA) Aspect Ratio: 1.37 : 1 Trapper Kit Carson and his band of men join John C. Fremont on his way to California. Enroute they are subjected to Indian attacks that are propagated by the Mexican Government, that does not want the contingency to reach California. Once in California, Fremont and Carson initiate a campaign to free the state from Mexican control.

sources.title: Kit Carson (1940)
sources.description: Frontiersman Kit Carson fights off Indian attacks on the trail to California. Watch on RetroFlix:  https://retroflix.org/kit-carson-1940/

sources.title: Kit Carson 1940 colorized (Jon Hall)
sources.description: Frontier scout Kit Carson and his band of men escort a wagon train of settlers led by John C. Fremont along the Oregon Trail to California, but they are attacked by Indians given rifles by the government of Mexico, which does not want them getting to California and agitating for it to become an independent state. Jon Hall as Kit Carson Lynn Bari as Dolores Murphy Dana Andrews as Capt. John C. Fremont Harold Huber as Lopez Ward Bond as Ape Renie Riano as Miss Pilchard Clayton Moore as Paul Terry Rowena Cook as Alice Terry Raymond Hatton as Jim Bridger Stanley Andrews as Larkin Colorized by Color Systems Technology

metadata.Plot: Trapper Kit Carson and his band of men join John C. Fremont on his way to California. Enroute they are subjected to Indian attacks that are propagated by the Mexican Government, that does not want the contingency to reach California. Once in California, Fremont and Carson initiate a campaign to free the state from Mexican control.
metadata.Actors: Jon Hall, Lynn Bari, Dana Andrews
